## GAUGE chart, OBEYA style

Render as SVG (or equivalent) on a 960x540 px canvas.
Every position, size, and color below is exact. No adjectives, no improvisation.

### AI metadata (for agent routing)
```json
{
  "code": "CH-MAG-09",
  "ovp_version": "1.0",
  "intent": [
    "magnitude",
    "status"
  ],
  "form": "semicircle dial: banded scale, needle at value, target tick",
  "data_required": "ONE value against a min-max scale with declared bands and a target",
  "family": "MAG",
  "alternatives": [
    "CH-MAG-04 (several metrics, honest bands)",
    "CH-PTW-06 (share of 100)"
  ],
  "see_instead": [
    {
      "when": "more than one metric",
      "use": "CH-MAG-04"
    },
    {
      "when": "no dial expectation exists",
      "use": "CH-MAG-04"
    }
  ]
}
```

Use when:
- WARN: only when a dashboard convention demands a dial for ONE number
- single watched value with declared bands and target

Do NOT use when:
- any multi-metric view (a gauge burns a page on one number)
- audiences without the dial convention (bullet reads faster)

  "max": "scale end",

}

### Rules
- bands are the deterministic ink-mix ramp, same as CH-MAG-04
- the value is printed large: the needle decorates, the number informs
- target drawn as a tick crossing the band

### Never do this
- WARN ENTRY: a gauge spends maximal ink on one number; CH-MAG-04 shows five in the same space
- unlabeled bands
- 3D dials
- needles without printed values

### QA before delivering
- [ ] value printed large
- [ ] bands ascending and declared
- [ ] min <= target, value <= max
- [ ] source present

### Colors (resolved, use these exact values)

| Element | Color |
|---|---|
| background | `#FFFFFF` |
| needle | `#0F172A` |
| target | `#B91C1C` |
| value | `#0F172A` |
| tick_label | `#334155` |
| title | `#0F172A` |
| source | `#334155` |

### Fonts

- Title font: 'Segoe UI',Arial,sans-serif, weight 700
- All other text: 'Segoe UI',Arial,sans-serif, weight 600 (600 on highlighted items)

### Language chrome

No kicker, no eyebrow; title at the default y.
